evidence: We are devoting significant capital and management time and resources to using AI technologies to improve our products, services and controls, and to build and expand our capabilities.
We have devoted and plan to continue devoting substantial time, money and management resources to developing new technologies, products and services.
we have been on a multi-year evolution to modernize our Marketplace platforms... using advanced technology, such as AI and Gen AI
using Generative AI (Gen AI) to prefill item details, generate optimized titles and descriptions and in some cases recognize product images enabling faster, more complete listings.

Our AI-enhanced features and services power dynamic product recommendation feeds... as well as our AI shopping agent pilot that delivers personalized search results tailored to individual preferences.
AI-enhanced discovery tools and interactive live shopping experiences through eBay Live... one-click card grading and resale for trading cards through our partnership with PSA

EU AI Act... non-compliance can result in fines up to 35 million euros or 7% of global turnover... AI regulation is also expanding in the United States, with AI-focused laws expected to take effect in 2026.

AI enhanced products could produce inaccurate, misleading, biased or false outcomes (e.g., hallucinatory behavior currently seen with Gen AI models) or other unexpected results that could harm our reputation.
hackers have unsuccessfully targeted us using an AI-generated voice impersonation of our CEO. We expect cyberattacks utilizing AI to continue and evolve.

limitations: No quantified dollar amounts or specific budget figures for AI investment are disclosed in the excerpts; investment intensity scored 3 rather than 4/5 due to absence of quantified spend.

ai_narrative_concreteness scored 4 (deployed with operating detail) based on named production features; no quantified business results (e.g., % uplift, volume metrics) were found to justify a score of 5.

capex_mentions list is empty as no specific dollar or capacity figures tied to AI/compute investment appear in the excerpts; a useful-life change saving $66M in depreciation was noted but relates to general server/networking equipment, not AI-specific capex.
